That was like the last of my money This guy's selling everyone in here $2,000 product and as soon as he closed he finished his presentation I looked and people started popping up from all around this room made about 500 people in the room and they're popping up And I'll start running towards the back.
I thought this this is fascinating.
I can't believe what's happening I looked in the back and I started counting as counting the people my 2004 6 8 10 I was doing the math.

I'm a very shy person But I learned how to be a speaker and learn how to present the right way because I wanted to make money Okay, I'm a capitalist just like all of us right.

I like these guys aren't even teaching that that good of stuff I remember being frustrated and so finally Then my very first chance I had to speak on stage I got all excited and what I went up there and I had all my content laid out I had like all my best stuff all my best ideas that I never had put in this presentation And I wanted to wow the audience I went up there and I shared with them so much stuff way more than anybody else had ever Had I ever seen give it an event and I was like these guys are gonna love me so much They are gonna come and buy my stuff and I just blew their minds with with all this just awesomeness and guess what happened nothing Nobody moved I closed at the end and I sold my product Nobody went to the back of the room and it was embarrassing I literally standing up on stage watch nobody pop up nobody moved and I then quietly walked to the back of the audience And people kind of shuffled out and left for lunch and it was the most awkward worst feeling I've ever felt in my life I was so embarrassed I literally walked out of that room walked to my hotel room and I Shut the door and for three days the rest of it.
I sat in the room.

Okay, and this is the same script I use when I'm speaking from stage to sell as they do on a webinar But on a webinar people can't see you Okay, so the biggest thing is like your tonality in your voice is what keys people around if you talk like this and you're you're kind of quiet Nobody will buy your product.
Okay.
If you look at me right now I'm literally sitting on the edge of my seat.
I have my hands in front of me and I'm moving them around And I my voice goes up really really high and it goes down and I have to have tonality have to be exciting I have to get people engaged.
Okay, if you watch my webinars, you notice that what's interesting is um I learned this first time when I was uh, I we did an infomercial a little while ago and uh for the infomercial I remember Um the guy the infomercial producer told me said look Russell if you talk like you normally do on tv You're gonna sound like this He said if you talk super excited and kind of hypey it over the top You're gonna sound normal and it's the same on a webinar You have to come with more energy than you do in your real world Otherwise people are gonna zone out and they're gonna fall asleep.
Okay, so I'm like at the level 10 right now Just talking to you guys.
I'm not even selling you something right now But if I was it's even more important.
Okay, so starting with a lot of energy and being exciting Usually about a minute or two before the webinar starts.

So it's kind of my intro now.
I lead initially with a bold promise Okay, this bold promise is going to tie back here to what I call the one thing but the bold promise is kind of like um Uh, I I call it a ruler.
Okay, it's it's like someone's everyone's measuring you all the time, right?
And if I come on a webinar, I start teaching they're going to measure me versus every other webinar They've ever been on in the past and they've been on people that are awesome on webinars.
That's what they're going to judge me against That's what the ruler is that they're going to say.
Was that a good webinar or bad?
It's all going to be based on the ruler.
They're using okay, and so that I don't want to have them judge me based on just my presentation skills alone because again, I'm going to talk too fast I'm gonna see a lot of ums.
I'm going to slur things Um, I want to judge.
I want them to judge me based on what I'm promising.

Okay at the end of this webinar I'm gonna you're going to learn exactly how to do that and if so then this webinar will be a benefit success Okay, boom big huge promise and that's not what they're judging it on They're judging the entire webinar based on if this this promise.
I just made them if it's true Okay, then if they're like, yeah, Russell taught me taught me exactly how to build a sales funnel without personally talking on the phone Anyone ever boom.

I just got the hard way, but you're gonna have a chance to cheat Uh, again, here's the hard way.
I did it.
It was really hard and frustrating Um, boom and then I this is where I switched to the future pacing Okay, I say look So future pacing guys, you know future pacing is I try to put them in a state where uh, they can visual They can visualize what will happen when they've accomplished this thing Okay, so if you ever heard me if you ever heard me say on a webinar or anything When I'm like, can you guys imagine what's going to be like someday?
When your business is up and running and you're making your first thousand dollars like, wouldn't that be awesome?
So I'm doing I'm doing future pacing.
Okay, it's an l it's a it's a concept from nlp Where I'm trying to take you to a place in the future where you've already achieved your goals Okay, so if I can do that I get it's part of the intro.
I'm like, so right here I said, look, do you guys want an easy way that's going to require you to never pick up a phone?
I can imagine that like making high ticket sales out every afternoon pick up the phone ever Like what would you do with your life?
What would you do different?
Okay, what would in a month from now when you're doing that?
Like what what's it going to be like?
What's your what's your spouse going to say?
What are your kids going to think when they're all of a sudden their dad's at home And they're making twice as much money.
It's pretty exciting.
Don't you think?
Okay, boom.
I'm future pacing them.
All right pushing them out into the future All right, then I kind of go back.
This is just more Again, the script is the script is is a framework and I add things in between that just I want to right sometimes So this one I just kind of bragged a little bit last 14 days did over six figures in high ticket products I didn't pick up the phone and then I like throwing in some kind of like fTC disclaimer like results are not typical This is not going to work.
Blah blah blah just to keep yourself kind of safe and now I shift over here to To the content part of the webinar.
Okay Now I want to step back One of the biggest problems that people make when they're creating a webinar or any sales piece for that matter.
It can be sales videos sales letters webinars everything is Because they're so in love with their product or their service and there's so many facets of it They they try to they try to like sell something at every single thing that their thing can do right all the different all the big All the big uh the big benefits of it.
In fact, um one of our inner circle members I was just reviewing his sales video and he's crushing They're doing I think it did a million bucks last year with their sales video But I said the biggest problem with your sales video right now The reason why you're converting it one or two percent versus five or six percent is because you're selling four or five different things People have to believe five different things you want to buy your product Okay, I learned this originally from peri belcher.
He talked about he said that when he had sales letters That that tried to get people to believe in two or more things they always flopped He said he said if I had sales letters that just tried to get somebody to believe one thing one core thing If I did that then they were successful Okay, and I saw the exact same thing happen to me you guys I cannot tell you how many times all the webinars i've done when I try to like okay to get somebody to buy this They need to believe in this this and this so I structure where i'm trying to teach them all these things And you can't do it that way that'll destroy your sales Okay, it's the way the reason why when I first did this it didn't work I get on stage I'm gonna show you my seven best things at how I make money I show up seven things and they're like wow, I got a lot of value out of that But I got seven things I gotta go do now and they would never buy right So you have to figure out from from your entire presentation from everything you do.
What's the one thing?
Okay, what is that one thing that is is going to change and just make Like like the one thing they got to have they have that one thing It'll all make sense.
Okay, if you look back here to my title.
This was my one thing Okay, one thing I learned from dagen smith was powerfully said that if you're one thing your one thing should always be something like this So this should be how to blank without blank Okay, how to and then insert like what they want Okay, so for the audience I was talking to they all want high ticket sales Everyone online right now is talking about high ticket sales.
So how to get how to instantly add high ticket sales to your sales funnel Okay, that's what they want But what what do they not want?
Okay for my audience?

I'm gonna teach you right now called the stack It was before I spoke my first time speaking arman's event And it was two weeks before we were at a dad kennedy event arman got there He spoke to an audience of thousand people and he closed like I don't know It was insane like 30 or 40 or 50 of the audience they ran back and he did like I don't know It was just shy of a million dollars in sales by himself And I remember watching I was just like that was the coolest thing ever and afterwards he came up to me With this huge stack of order forms in his hands He's like you want a bunch of order forms like this next week or two weeks when you speak at my event Like yeah, man, I totally do like what I got to do and he said I got to teach us the process called the stack.
He's like if you do it, you'll get a big stack of order forms You don't do it.
You're gonna not can make much money.
It's like that's just how it works.
I'm like, all right Well, how's it work?
He said this is the big secret He said most of the presenters you watch the way they do their presentation They'll say hey the first thing you're gonna get is this and then you're gonna get this and then you're gonna get this He keeps walking through all the stuff they're gonna get and then the last slide They're like this is bonus number 12 this we're gonna get and then go over They go over to the next slide.
We're like I'm gonna give you all the stuff for you know, $10,000 And then they start doing the price drop now What happens he says the only thing that the human mind remembers is the last thing they were showed so Right before you say right before you show them this big price The only thing they remember is bonus number 10 or 12 whatever your last thing is you showed and that bonus doesn't justify that price on their mind Then they're not gonna buy it He said so I invented this thing called the stack is this how it works You can kind of see here.
He says I start my presentation I stack like like the the first component and I talk about it and then Actually, I'll show you guys how it works down here.
So when I did when I did the stack first thing It is I show right here I said, let me talk about what you're gonna get.
Okay first thing you're gonna get is this So I do the first stack second thing you're gonna do is you're gonna get this And third thing you're gonna do is this okay If I was if I was clicking through these are animated fourth thing you're gonna do is this boom So this is the total value 8,000 bucks.
So this is the core offer.
Okay, so if any of you guys um If any of you guys are uh Like if you have your core thing you're teaching somebody So because it's gonna be six modules or it's gonna be, you know, four modules each and blah, blah, blah, blah That's what this was.
This is my core my core offer, right?
I'm gonna set up a two step.

I'm gonna give you the actual script for you I'm gonna give you robbie in a box.

Everybody knew exactly what the offer was and they bought like crazy So I don't not ever do this because it works so good.

Okay guys um Those who are in Boise Natasha Natasha was there and I taught Natasha the stack probably Man probably two years ago and uh, she's one of my favorite people she learned something she applies it She did the first webinar and she crushed it and she did again.

I used to sell her high-end coaching And she did the stack and she actually had huge uh huge boards Up on the wall it had the stack everything was kind of covered up and one time she pulled the thing off Pulled the second thing off and she did the whole thing and boom She did her stack and she made she sold uh 10 people at five grand 50 or $50,000 in sales from one presentation To a group of like 30 people by using the stack and she swears by she won't ever not do it I'm the same way.
Okay after arm and showed to me I added my presentation I went did his event and boom I crushed I was the number one salesperson the event I walked away with like almost a hundred grand in sales and I was like this thing is awesome And I just have used it every single time Religiously since then actually that's not true once I thought I was too smart and I didn't use it and I bombed I still remember the webinar.
I was so embarrassed Um, I was doing it for some guy and I I thought I had a better idea than the stack and I did it and webinar bombed I've never I've put it back in I've never not done it since then Okay, so that's the webinar works you guys here's component number one boom talk about component one Here's component number two Which means you're gonna get this and this talk about that, you know add testimonials these little spiny faces are Our bonuses testimonials inserting closes just how much the stuff to strengthen fortified that next component then you restack it Share the next thing Restack it share the next thing restack it and then boom after you restack it all now as we come to the big price Okay, that means that total price of all this stuff What did I do that on?
The total price is Somewhere boom Okay, the total price is 11,998 dollars.
I show the big huge price now most people they do that and they get scared like Oh, but don't worry guys.
I'm not gonna charge you that And what happens you just lost your credibility That's one of the biggest mistakes.
I see I see Webinar people do they show the big price and then they freak out.
Don't worry guys.
I'm not gonna charge you that And they move on That's like the worst thing And this is again this attack I learned from from Dustin and Dave is as soon as I revealed a huge price, right?
I show the 11,000 dollar thing now.
I need to make that price I need to justify that price in their head if I don't justify that price in their head Then they're not then then when I actually sell the product it's not gonna work I have to get them mentally to believe that it's actually worth that And if I don't then I lost the sale Okay, so after I show this high ticket thing this now is where This is where I actually Added my if-all statements.
Okay.
I learned this from from Dave van Hoos So I I I set the high price and I say look guys I'm not I'm not gonna charge 11,998 dollars today.
I could but I'm not going to but I want to ask you something And then this is where you add your if-all if all that this package did was Blank would it be worth it?
If all this if if all this package did was finally make your business profitable your business profitable Would it be worth 11,000 bucks?
Yeah, I thought so Now if all this package did was finally give you the kick in the butt you need to get moving would it be worth it?
I think so too Now if all this package did was give you two to three high ticket clients per month that'd be worth it Yeah, I don't know about you, but I I'd trade 12,000 dollars for two or three You know five to ten thousand dollar sales month all day long.
This is one time investment You do it once you never have to pay again, but that knowledge the information everything you got will pay you forever Okay, so these if all statements they solidify this price in their mind That makes sense you guys if all And now I come back say but don't worry guys.
I'm not going to charge you that In fact, what I'm going to do is and you start dropping the price Okay, I went a little bit further because I talked about about two three clients What's that worth to him and I said and I want to ask you another question Like what's one client going to be worth to you?
If your package at five grand it's worth five thousand ten thousand twenty five thousand more So how much would you pay to get one high ticket client per month?

I'm gonna I'm gonna move on to this.
I'll start disposable income I'll come back that when I find that one disposable income is one of my one of my favorites So if you come up here, you'll see where I use disposable income Disposable income okay, so disposable income this is where um you you talk about The the fact that like like all of us have disposable income like most you guys You get your paycheck each month and a percentage that goes towards Towards movies and towards things like that and uh, and I'm not asking you guys to to go and go into depth for this at all What I'm trying to ask you guys to do is that some of that disposable income that you're gonna spend on the movies and And and going out to dinner and all that kind of stuff instead of blowing it on something That's just going to be there and then kind of fade away and you're gonna lose it You I'm asking you to make an investment yourself because the money you put into this is going to turn into more money Okay, a lot of times like talking about I might talk about Robert Kiyosaki here Like you guys have read the book rich dad poor dad now in the book rich dad poor dad He talked about difference between assets and liabilities.
He said that the poor people they buy liabilities Okay, they buy cars they buy they buy boats they buy these things you put money in and the money disappears Okay, um what the rich people do is they put their money into investments that actually turn into more money So all I'm doing is ask you to divert some of your disposable income into an into an investment As opposed to something that's going to take money away from you.
Okay, that's kind of how I use How I do the disposable income close.
Okay money replenish this close this one is One of my my other favorite ones.
So money replenishes basically um, the the concept is Is look every single month you get a paycheck in fact, I wonder if I where I put this one in here Um Might have been a find on the fly so as you can see though, I sprinkle these out throughout the stack And I'm not gonna find it real quick.
Anyway money replenishes.
So the basis like look, this is how it works Every two weeks you get paid a paycheck, right?
And the cool thing about it is you you get that paycheck you go out you pay your bills You buy you know your disposable income you spell it money and the money disappears, right?
But what happens two weeks later?
You get paid again that money replenishes and the cool thing about money is money always replenishes Okay, every two weeks it comes back again every two weeks comes back again.
He's coming back in over and over and over again, right?
Let me ask you a question does that happen with your time too?
Like when you when you uh, you miss your your daughter's soccer games.
You're at the office like you get that time back No, it doesn't huh?
It's not weird like money replenishes every single two weeks, but but your time never comes back So i'm asking you to do is to take a take an asset take a resource that replenishes every two weeks It's gonna come back you're gonna get that money back every two weeks And invest in something that's gonna save you a ton of time because you can learn this stuff You can go do what I did you can go hire 60 salespeople You can go and build your own call center you can build a sales team you can hire you can do all stuff But i'm trying to do with this package that I created for you guys I'm just trying to save you a bunch of time, okay?
And because you can't get your time back.

It's magic.
Okay, so that's a really good one and the last one This one's kind of a bigger thing kind of a higher an overall hierarchy principle I learned this initially from a guy named ted thomas They call him the pied piper because when he speaks at like a robert kiyosaki event I've seen pictures of him He'll literally be walking around the room and he'll have a line of like 500 people all holding their credit card falling in behind him waving their credit cards and they'll fall into the back of the room and uh It's uh, it's pretty awesome.
Anyway, they call him the pied piper because that and I met him at an event He saw me speak and then afterwards he pulled me inside.
He said rustle.
You did you did great You did sales were good.
He said, do you notice one thing? he said when when you were uh When you were speaking so I was sitting in the back of the audience and you did a great job You know people signed up.
They're excited, but I noticed nobody's heads are moving You said when you come to my audience and you watch me speak you said and you're sitting in the back of the room It looks like you're watching a wave so everybody's heads are nodding up and down the entire 90 minutes Okay, he said he said, you know how I do that.
I said no, how do you do?
He said I do it by this little thing called trial closes You know all the trial closes is is something you I throw out there to get somebody to say yes.
Does that make sense?
Okay, I said does that make sense because that was a trial close He knows probably knows I say that a lot.
Okay, I say isn't that awesome?
You guys getting this Is this exciting like when you see how this all works and and everyone listening at home saying yeah That is exciting.
That is cool.
I start nodding their head.
Yes.
Okay, so the first time I learned about these trial closes I went back to an automated webinar I had and I literally went through and I rewatched the entire like three hour long webinar And I listened to every single spot.
I could insert a trial close and I wrote this thing out and I had my brother record me Um, or I record myself saying each these little trial closes and I had my brother who does my video stuff Insert those trial closes in my entire three hour webinar and then we started running it and our Our profit per Per webinar registrant went from nine dollars and like 40 cents a person up to 16 dollars It was like almost we almost doubled our income just by adding in these trial closes.
It's crazy Okay, now for me when I first started doing this what I would do is I would take all these trial closes And I'd write them down on note cards and I put them all over my desk and I was giving webinars I looked down I would say him I would get to a spot.
I'd be like, isn't that awesome?
Are you guys getting this?
Okay, do you guys see how this would help you in your life?
Can you imagine what it'd be like if you were doing this every single day?
Okay, I started saying that all over the all over the place and now it's the point where if you guys just watch this Webinar I'm not even trying to close you by guarantee your head's been nodding I've been dropping trial closes every like 10 seconds on you guys.
It's just how I speak now, right?
And it's something you guys will learn as well But trial closes are powerful and best place to add trial closes Oh besides the entire presentation But anytime you share a success story or you share one of your big secrets Most people will share testimonial and then they move on.
